reviews.review_msg: A strict and harsh marker, and will be disrespectful to students. Beware
ABSOLUTE BADDIE. She truly does care about your success. Tough love that makes you lock in.
was a good lecturer and knew how to teach the material well
Wait until another prof teaches the course

Irina is the best prof I have ever had at SFU. She runs her class like a navy ship and is intimidating in a good way. I learned a lot in this course and feel like she was very clear. A bit blunt at times, but she is good at what she does.

(I took this class fall 2025) I think people misunderstand her, she was a bit scary to me too, but she honestly just seems to want to be fair (eg. People didn't clean despite reminders and were penalized) even then the class average was pretty high (88% befoee the final). Since her grading rubric was really straightforward and easy to understand.

Dr. K is amazing. Not only was she a great lecturer for a dense course but her exams are fair and she tells you exactly what is expected of you, she also graded and input our grades onto gosfu within a 3 day window. The class is huge and I know she also has another class also. It doesn't get more on the ball than that!

Irina is one of the best profs out there. She does care for teaching, as she wants us to constantly keep up with the course load. She gave us in person lecture prompts and then uploaded it to canvas, tutorial quiz answers were uploaded, and a bunch of practice that came in handy for the final. Final was super easy and overall I am going to miss her

Dr. Kovalyova is amazing, she is caring and wants you to do well, she also provides you with everything you need to succeed. She can come off as tough but that sass also makes her hilarious, her lectures are interesting and she has all sorts of analogies to help you remember, she also tends to emphasize certain things many times. Highly recommend!

Dr. Kovalyova is an amazing and caring professor. Although MBB 231 is a content-heavy course, she made a real effort to simplify the material and explained topics clearly. The tests were fair and reflected what was taught in class. I truly enjoyed taking this course with her

Dr. Kovalyova is an amazing prof. She has amazing analogies that help you understand the content and her slides are very simple but informative enough to understand. She's a little sassy and sarcastic, that could be misinterpreted as being rude, but honestly i think she's hilarious. Lots of hw but it definitely helps you prepare for the final

Dr. K can be strict but it helps you learn. She is compassionate and approachable if you go talk to her in office hours. Exams were fair, nothing unexpected. You have to go to class as there are participation marks.

Very harsh and unsympathetic instructor. Even when reached out with issues, the replies felt cold and invalidated. she decides what she thinks of you, that's the tone for the rest of the course. Asking for help  felt almost impossible. If you need any level of understanding or basic flexibility, this class will be extremely stressful. badly taught.

Decent prof. She does seem to hate the students unintentionally, doesn't really give much leeway for being late to class even if you have a previous class, and kicking someone out on the 2nd lecture didn't feel good. Her test's difficulty isnt super hard. Run-of-the-mill, go to class, tutorials, and office hours.
She can be very rude at times. She doesn't help a lot in the labs. Overall, the class seems to be fair. If you are good at lab work then you will be fine.

Worst prof I have ever had. She rushes you during labs for her own benefit, and is very passive aggressive towards students she doesn't like. She is very hot and cold in the sense that she can be kind at times and very very rude and disrespectful at other times. Even if you work hard, it's so hard to improve if she doesn't like you.
Tough teacher, hard to do well if she dislikes you

Dr. K is tough and strict but this is for your own benefit. Her leading her classes in this way ensure you actually stay on top of things because the course is content dense. She's also extremely kind, compassionate and approachable. I think she is probably the best prof in the MBB department. Put in the work, as you should! She's great and fair!!
She is quite difficult but very knowledgeable, enthusiastic and approachable. Quizzes are hard. She is strict but extremely fair. Put in the work and you will be fine.
She is the only one who teaches this class, unlucky. Go to class and pray she doesn't single you out
My favorite MBB course. It's hard and you have to put the work in. Honestly not for everyone. I recommend taking 229 first which helped me alot. Get ready to work.

Would not recommend taking if you ever want clarification or asking for help. She will not help you. She was disrespectful to me and actually laughed in front of the whole class over something that I didn't even do.
Quizzes were hard
Dr. K seriously wants you to succeed. She is strict but she is fair. I will definitely take more courses with her.

Dr. K was helpful in that she was quite involved in lab, ensuring she rotated throughout labs to check in. She's a tough marker and her exams are quite difficult but not the worst prof but not the best either. As a person though, Dr. K was amusing to talk to and kind.
Dr. K is the analogy queen. She makes you understand.
Dr. K is an awesome prof - she is approachable, uses good analogies, and wants you to do well in the class. She is a strict marker but she is fair. I will definitely take more courses with her.
Not recommended at all she is scary
Do not take, wait for another teacher to teach the class. If not, look for other class alternatives.
Hope for another prof, strongly not recommended
Irina is extremely unhelpful and will disregard you when asking questions during lab. She will ignore you or tell you that you should already know this and not say a word.

Had Dr. K for 331 and 309W. Be ready to get to work. Be prepared and you will be rewarded. If you think you didn't prepare well enough the first time, reach out and ask questions. She's super accessible over email and is welcoming to questions. She may seem tough and strict, but she wants you to do well and learn from your mistakes.
Do not recommend
Awesome prof. Get ready to work. Show up prepared or you will embarrass yourself!
Had Dr. K for 222 and 309 and hope to have her for more of my courses. Expect to work hard. Best proff I have had in MBB. MBB is not for everyone.
Dr. K is friendly and helpful. Show up prepared and you will succeed. Go to office hours!!! Best lab course I have taken.
The class isn't hard but Irina makes it over complicated. She is unapproachable and disrespectful.

309 is a challenging course but Dr. K was an engaging and helpful professor. Her labs abd exams were hard but reasonable if you prepared and studied. Averages were fair and she was very knowledgable in lab. She expects you to show up to labs prepared in order to do well. Choose partners wisely.
Class was brutal and she doesn't make it any better. Don't know how anyone can like her
I am just so intimidated asking her any questions during the lab. She will straight up not help or insult you before helping.
Not friendly nor approachable, but unwillingly helps by belittling you for asking her questions. Not recommended
I am just so scared of her.. she is definitely not approachable and makes my day hard.

She expects us to know how to submit our assignments when there is no explanation on how to do so. Our critical reviews are to be handed in paper but there are no instruction or announcement where or how the 11:59 deadline makes sense. She makes the peer review for our papers due the day before our final critical review, so we have 1 day to revise.
Avoid, she insults her students and thinks she is better than them. Assumes they dont know anything

Taking the notoriously difficult MBB 331 course with Dr. K was challenging yet rewarding. Graded quizzes are given on paper after each lecture. You may hate this idea but will feel rewarded if you take it seriously and ask questions. The exams were very frustrating and roughly graded, but luckily, they were not the only place to get marks.

Never felt more discouraged in my life. In-class quizzes after every lecture for attendance marks based on the material she teaches that day and mandatory attendance in tutorials. Unlimited cheat sheets for all exams but it won't help you. Exams are all concepts that are not taught. Future students, STUDY THE PRACTICE EXAMS, NOT THE LECTURE SLIDES.

Honestly, if you get your money's worth from tuition and actually attend her lectures, she'll tend to *really* emphasize certain things that are examinable. There is a LOT of material in this course, so I can kinda see why she can sometimes seem rushed, but she was very approachable after lecture and really good at clarifying things I didn't get

I had Dr K for 229 and 222. She's the best prof i have had in MBB. Go to lectures and go to office hours. She can seem intimidating at first but she's so knowledgable in her field and soooo helpful if you just go and ask. Can not wait for 309 with her. Take her courses!
She is the best prof I've had in mbb. Go to office hours. Tells you whats on exams if you go to lecture. Take her courses!

Tbh she's not bad. Just really have to memorize every key detail of each lecture. Exams make are entirely your grade so do your best on the first two midterms. Final exam is not culumative even tho she says it is! Focus on the last few lectures and you should be ok.

Lecture slides are very text heavy yet do not have most of the important information on them (she will make additional notes on slides which you will not have access to). She does not record lectures or let you have your phone out to record lectures. So if you miss a day, you miss all the important information. Exams are VERY specific.

I really like her teaching style, it is straightforward and facilitates understanding. She is very strict but reasonable. Labs are long with some done in groups so choose wisely. There are weekly lab quizzes and she expects you to know lab content in detail (fair but time consuming).
such boring lectures.

I do not think this professor should be teaching in all honesty. There are many reasons but based on the guidelines set out by rate my professor I cannot expand. All I can say is that I do not recommend taking her.

Dr. K is awesome, she knows her stuff and explains things clearly. Great prof, would def take again. 

for the course itself: Lab reports are LONG and 2 are done in groups .... if you get a bad group sucks to be you. Quizzes are fair.
